item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me of a story I saw on the twilight zone. I cant remember the name of the episode but I know it was about a man who couldn't control his hand.
I think it would be pretty easy to solve this mystery with an autopsy on the dead wife. Based on her injuries I think it would be possible to know if she were strangled by his right or left hand.
It's a clever idea having alien body syndrome rather than alien hand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It took the bodysnatcher/copycat trope, stuck it in one body, and I’m not sure who’s lying or telling the truth. That was enough of a hook for me to upvote/do a reading. Good job.

https://youtu.be/DXBKaylkn6I

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This falls a bit too much into "note at the end" like Roget says, and furthermore the note itself is a bit flowery for a desperate person.

However I did like the twist which changed the way you think about the anomaly vs normalcy. Overall enjoyed it enough for an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a fun idea and I think it has a lot of potential, but I'm disappointed by the article as it stands now. It didn't even seem to be all that anomalous to me until we got to the note. The Split-Up and to a lesser extent Your Leg show limb articles with the level of development this needs. As it stands, most of this article is quite boring and I can't bring myself to upvote for a decent note at the end.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
You know, it's kinda funny. I have a lot of stuff on my plate right now, and I have been working on some of that stuff for months now. But two nights ago, I just looked at my own hand in the bathroom mirror and wiggled my fingers, and came up with this idea on the spot. And now it's here. I know it is very short, but it does what it's supposed to. If it does that well, well, that's up for you, the readers, to decide. At least I feel like this helped me refine my writing process. Also, Jam Con is coming up, so part of this was practicing writing something short and concise in a short amount of time.

Thanks to CorpseOfBixby, H0dari, and AlanDaris for critting this.
